claimText: Bahrain, Colombia, the Democratic Republic of Congo, Latvia, and Liberia were recently elected to serve as non-permanent members of the United Nations Security Council.
evidenceExcerpt: Five countries, namely Bahrain, Colombia, the Democratic Republic of Congo, Latvia, and Liberia, were recently elected to serve as non-permanent members of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the recently elected non-permanent members of the UNSC.
distractorHooks: Replacing one of the countries with a different country from the same region
Claiming these countries were elected as permanent members
